title: MCP Tool Manifest
description: Contract 4 — MCP server manifest returned by every MCP server's `/manifest` endpoint. Naming: server `name` uses dash-case (tool-web-search); individual tool `name` uses snake_case (web_search) to match MCP's JSON-RPC method-name convention. Manifests target MCP protocol v1; `schema_version` is this contract's version, `protocol_version` is the MCP wire-protocol version. Scope granularity enforced before invocation: coarse `tool:invoke` (any tool), fine `tool:<server-name>:invoke` (specific server), wildcard `tool:*:invoke` (admin/platform only). Tool-declared `timeout_seconds` is the hard ceiling; effective per-call timeout = min(A2A_remaining, tool.timeout_seconds). Request-style schema: strict, but tolerates unknown OPTIONAL keys inside `input_schema`/`output_schema` (which are nested JSON Schemas).

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this contract schema, e.g. "1.0.0".

name.description: MCP server name in dash-case, e.g. "tool-web-search".

version.description: Semantic version of the MCP server implementation, e.g. "1.2.0".

description.description: One-line description of what the server does.

author.description: Publishing author or team, e.g. "CypherX Platform".

auth_required.description: Whether a Bearer token is required to invoke this server's tools.

required_scopes.description: Scopes required to invoke this server, e.g. ["tool:invoke", "tool:tool-web-search:invoke"].

input_schema.description: JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) describing the tool's input. Nested schema; unknown JSON Schema keywords tolerated.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) describing the tool's output. Nested schema; unknown JSON Schema keywords tolerated.

timeout_seconds.description: Hard ceiling for a single tool invocation, in seconds. xAgent rejects effective timeouts above this.

idempotent.description: Whether re-invoking with identical input is side-effect free.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Estimated cost in USD per invocation.

$comment: additionalProperties:true on the manifest object, each tool, and rate_limit is intentional and required for forward-compatibility — Phase 7 (Tools) extends manifests with new fields (capability layer, sandbox_class, egress allowlist, tenant-tool metadata). Older validators MUST tolerate unknown fields; field-level patterns/enums still validate known fields.
